description: Change Mode (chmod): Essential Linux utility for modifying file systems access permissions (read, write, execute) for owners, groups, and global audiences.

commands.explanation: Grants absolute read (4), write (2), and execute (1) permissions to Owner, Group, and Anyone perfectly. Highly insecure for regular usage.
Grants Owner absolute rights (7) while Group and Others can strictly only read and execute (5). Standard for web server files.
Sets permissions so strictly that the file is only readable and writable by the direct Owner (6). Perfect for SSH private keys.
Universally adds the 'execute' permission logic natively allowing all users to run this binary or script.
Explicitly assigns User/Owner (rwx), Group (rw-), and Others (r--) via explicit symbolic representation rather than math.
Specifically subtracts/removes write permissions exclusively from the Group assigned to this file.
Recursively sweeps through a directory assigning the 755 permission structure to all sub-directories and embedded files.
Sets the SUID (Set-user Identification) bit. This enables anyone to execute the file pretending they hold the permissions of the file owner.
Sets the SGID bit on a directory. All new files created within this directory will inherently inherit the directory's group ownership.
Applies the Sticky Bit constraint. Inside this directory, files can only be genuinely deleted by their specific Owner or root.
